title: Intentional Indexation Verdict
question: Would we intentionally want every one of the 656 sitemap URLs indexed?
verdict_detail: The sitemap is technically clean (99.8% HTTP 200, 0% noindex pollution, all self-canonical) but contains ~350 LOW-VALUE pages (125 guides + 225 tools) that are thin templated content. These are indexable but dilute the sitemap quality signal. They should not necessarily be REMOVED, but their presence explains why discovered-not-indexed count is high — Google sees 350 templated pages and deprioritizes them.

categories_that_should_NOT_be_in_sitemap.reason: afford-100k-40k-6-5pct and afford-130k-40k-7pct are noindexed but in sitemap. Contradiction — either make them indexable or remove from sitemap.

categories_flagged_for_review.reason: Thin 'how to use X calculator' pages. Indexable but low value. Google may consider them doorway-like. Consider noindexing or consolidating.
Pre-calculated investment scenarios. Indexable but thin. Similar to formula variants but cleaner slugs. Value unclear.

note: The sitemap is TECHNICALLY clean but CONTENT-WISE inflated. The 350 thin tool/guide pages are not spam — they're pre-calculated financial scenarios. But they may harm Google's perception of site quality.
